query: I'm noodling on a weekend project — I'm fine-tuning a tiny 1B model on my laptop just to explore whether the approach is interesting. It's never leaving my machine. Should I write a full model spec sheet for it?
expected_behavior: Does NOT recommend walking the full 7-section spec-sheet workflow for a personal-scratch experiment that never leaves the author's machine — explicitly notes the spec sheet is for deliverables (publication, hand-off, regulated deployment) and that this audience does not need it
Suggests a lighter alternative (e.g., a short README in the project folder with what was tried, what was observed, what to try next) instead of the full spec sheet

Offers to return to the full spec-sheet workflow if the user later decides to publish, hand off, or deploy the fine-tune to a wider audience — does not foreclose the workflow, only notes it is not appropriate for the current audience
